w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: UniTracko
Legal & Policy Documents
Effective Date: 1 April 2026
Version:
.0 | April 2026 Edition
"We collect only what we need. We never sell it. We never spam you."
Documents Included:
1. Privacy Policy
2. Terms & Conditions
3. Cookie Policy
4. Disclaimer
5. Our Data Promise
6. Refund & Cancellation Policy
Regulatory Note — April 2026 | Version
Updated: DPDP Rules 2025 (notified 13 Nov 2025) — Phase 1 active, full compliance by 13 May 2027.
www.unitracko.com
privacy@unitracko.com
1. Privacy Policy
Last Updated: April 2026 | Effective Date: 1 April 2026 | Version
TL;DR — Plain English Summary
We collect only what we need to help you navigate admissions.
We never sell your data to colleges, advertisers, or anyone else.
We never share your phone number with third parties who will call you uninvited.
You can access, correct, or delete your data any time
no hoops, no guilt trips.
Questions? Email
privacy@unitracko.com
and a real person will respond.
1.1 About Us
UniTracko
is an education and career guidance platform helping students and parents navigate college admissions in India. We are operated by
Unitracko
Private Limited
, incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, India.
Registered Address:
1856, Tower 12A,
Purvanchal
Royal City, Greater Noida
, India
Data Contact:
privacy@unitracko.com
1.2 Applicable Law
This Privacy Policy is governed by:
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act) and the DPDP Rules, 2025 (notified by
MeitY
on 13 November 2025, operative in phases)
The Information Technology Act, 2000 and the IT (SPDI) Rules, 2011, which continue to apply alongside the DPDP framework
The Consumer Protection Act, 2019
DPDP Rollout Status — April 2026
Phase 1 (Active since 13 Nov 2025): Data Protection Board of India is established and operational.
Phase 2 (From 13 Nov 2026): Consent Manager registration framework becomes active.
Phase 3 (From 13 May 2027): Full substantive compliance
consent notices, data principal rights, breach reporting, and security safeguard obligations come into full force.
UniTracko
is proactively building compliance ahead of the May 2027 deadline.
1.3 What Personal Data We Collect
Data You Provide Directly
Full name, email address, and mobile number
Academic information: current class, stream, grades, target colleges, and career interests
Parent or guardian contact details (when voluntarily provided)
Messages and queries you submit via contact forms, chat, or email
Data We Collect Automatically
Device type, operating system, browser type, and IP address
Pages visited, features used, session duration, and navigation patterns
Referral source (how you found
UniTracko
Cookies and similar tracking technologies (see Section 3
Cookie Policy)
Data We Do NOT Collect
Government identification numbers (Aadhaar, PAN) unless legally required
Financial data beyond what payment processors handle independently
Biometric or health data
We apply the DPDP principle of data
minimisation
we collect only what is necessary for the stated purpose.
1.4 Why We Collect It
Purpose Limitation
Under the DPDP Act, we process personal data only for purposes that are lawful and for which we have your consent or a legitimate use. Our purposes are:
Creating and managing your
UniTracko
account
Personalising
admission guidance, recommendations, and planning tools
Sending you updates relevant to your admission timeline
only with your consent
Improving our platform based on
anonymised
usage analytics
Responding to your queries and support requests
Processing payments for paid plans
Complying with legal and regulatory obligations
We will not use your data for any purpose not listed above without first notifying you and, where required, obtaining your consent.
1.5 What We Will NEVER Do
Sell, rent, or trade your personal data to any third party
ever
Share your phone number with colleges, coaching
centres
, or any entity that will call you without your explicit permission
Use your data to serve you targeted advertisements from third parties

Share your data with any party outside India without your explicit consent (cross-border transfer restrictions apply under the DPDP Act; as of April 2026, no restricted country list has been published but we treat transfers conservatively)
Retain your data beyond the period necessary for the stated purpose
Use automated decision-making that significantly affects you without disclosure
1.6 Who We Share Data With
We share data strictly on a need-to-know basis with:
Cloud infrastructure providers (for secure hosting and data storage)
Analytics tools
only
anonymised
, aggregated data
Payment processors
governed by their own PCI-DSS compliant policies; we do not store card details
Legal or regulatory authorities
only when required by law or court order
All third-party service providers are bound by data processing agreements that prohibit them from using your data for their own purposes or sharing it further.
1.7 Consent
How We Get It and How You Withdraw It
Under the DPDP Act, consent must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous. We obtain consent through:
A clear opt-in at account registration
no pre-ticked boxes
Separate consent for optional communications (newsletters, alerts)
Plain-language notices before we collect any new category of data
You may withdraw consent at any time by:
Emailing
privacy@unitracko.com
Using the data settings in your
UniTracko
account (once available)
Withdrawing consent will not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out before withdrawal. It may affect your ability to use certain platform features.
1.8 Your Rights as a Data Principal
Under the DPDP Act, 2023, you have the following rights:
Right to access: Know what personal data we hold about you
Right to correction: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data
Right to erasure: Request deletion of your data (subject to legal retention obligations)
Right to grievance redressal: Raise a complaint with us
we must acknowledge within
48 -72 hours.
Right to nominate: Nominate a person to exercise your data rights on your behalf in case of incapacity
Right to complaint: Escalate unresolved grievances to the Data Protection Board of India (operational since November 2025)
To exercise any right, contact:
privacy@unitracko.com
with subject line 'Data Principal Request'.
1.9 Processing Data of Children

The DPDP Act defines a 'child' as a person under 18 years of age. However, the Act's consent requirement for children applies specifically where data is processed in a manner that may cause harm or involves tracking, profiling, or targeted advertising
for which parental or guardian consent is required.
For our platform:
Users under 18 must have a parent or guardian provide verifiable consent before we process their data
We do not engage in
behavioural
monitoring, profiling, or targeted advertising directed at minors
as prohibited under the DPDP Act
We do not collect data from children below 13 under any circumstances
Parents may contact
privacy@unitracko.com
to access, correct, or delete their child's data at any time
The DPDP Rules 2025 specify the mechanism for verifiable parental consent. We are implementing this as Phase 3 obligations approach the May 2027 deadline.
1.10 Data Retention
Active account data: Retained for as long as your account remains active
Deleted account data: Permanently erased within 30 days of deletion request
Support communications: Retained for 12 months
Anonymised
analytics data: Retained for up to 24 months
Financial/billing records: Retained for 7 years as required under applicable tax and accounting laws
Once the purpose for which data was collected is fulfilled, and no legal obligation requires retention, we will erase or
anonymise
the data.
1.11 Data Security
AES-256 encryption for data at rest
TLS 1.2+ encryption for all data in transit
Role-based access controls only
authorised
personnel can access user data
Regular security audits and penetration testing
Incident response protocol in place for data breaches

In the event of a personal data breach that is likely to cause harm to you, we will notify the Data Protection Board of India and, where required, notify affected users in accordance with our obligations under the DPDP Act.
1.12 Cross-Border Data Transfers

As of April 2026, the Central Government has not published a list of restricted countries under the DPDP Act. Transfers to all jurisdictions remain permissible under current law. However, where we use overseas service providers (such as cloud hosting), we ensure contractual safeguards are in place to protect your data.
We will update this section promptly if the government notifies any transfer restrictions.
1.13 Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect changes in our practices or applicable law. For material changes, we will notify you by email or in-app notice at least 14 days before the change takes effect. Continued use of the platform after that period constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.
All previous versions of this policy are available on request.
2. Terms & Conditions
Last Updated: April 2026 | Effective Date: 1 April 2026 | Version
TL;DR — Plain English Summary
Use
UniTracko
in good faith, for your own admission planning.
You must be 13+ to create an account. Under-18s need parental consent.
We own our content. Don't scrape or reproduce it.
We don't guarantee admission to any college
that's the institution's call.
Indian law governs these terms.
2.1 Acceptance of Terms
By accessing or using the
UniTracko

platform ('Platform'), you agree to be bound by these Terms & Conditions ('Terms'), our Privacy Policy, and all other policies incorporated by reference. If you do not agree, please do not use the Platform. These Terms apply to all users
students, parents, and visitors.
2.2 Eligibility
You must be at least 13 years of age to create a
UniTracko
account
Users under 18 years of age must have verifiable parental or guardian consent
consistent with the DPDP Act, 2023 and DPDP Rules, 2025
You must provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and keep it updated
UniTracko
reserves the right to refuse or terminate accounts that do not meet eligibility requirements
2.3 Your Account
You are solely responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials
You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account
Notify us immediately at
contact@unitracko.com
if you suspect
unauthorised
access
Do not share your account with others or use another user's account
2.4 Permitted Use
UniTracko
is intended for personal, non-commercial use only. You may:
Research colleges, universities, and career paths
Build and track your
personalised
admission plan
Access guidance resources for academic and career decisions
Communicate with our support team
2.5 Prohibited Conduct
You must NOT use
UniTracko
to:
Impersonate any person, entity, or institution
Upload false, misleading, defamatory, or harmful content
Scrape, copy, or reproduce platform content or data without written permission
Attempt to hack, reverse-engineer, or disrupt the platform or its infrastructure
Use automated bots, crawlers, or scripts to access the platform
Engage in commercial solicitation, spam, or multi-level marketing
Upload malware, viruses, or any code designed to damage or compromise the platform
Attempt to access data of other users without
authorisation
Violation of any of the above may result in immediate account suspension and potential legal action.
2.6 Intellectual Property
All content on
UniTracko
including text, graphics, videos, tools, databases, software, UI design, and branding
is the exclusive property of
UniTracko
and protected under the Copyright Act, 1957, Trade Marks Act, 1999, and other applicable Indian intellectual property laws.
You may not reproduce, redistribute, publicly display, modify, create derivative works from, or commercially exploit any
UniTracko
content without express prior written consent.
User-submitted content (such as queries or feedback) may be used by
UniTracko
to improve the platform, in
anonymised
form only.
2.7 No Guarantee of Admission Outcomes
This clause is critical. Please read carefully.
UniTracko
provides planning tools, guidance, and information to support your admission journey. We explicitly disclaim any guarantee or assurance regarding:
Admission to any college, university, institution, or academic
programme
Accuracy of cut-off data, eligibility criteria, seat availability, or fee structures published by third-party institutions or governing bodies
Career outcomes, earning potential, or employability following any course or
programme
Success in any competitive examination (JEE, NEET, CUET, CAT, etc.)
All admission decisions rest entirely with the respective educational institutions, their governing bodies, and applicable counselling authorities.
UniTracko
is a guidance tool
not a decision-maker.
2.8 Third-Party Links & Services
The Platform may contain links to third-party websites, including official college portals, government websites, and news sources.
UniTracko
is not responsible for the accuracy, availability, privacy practices, or content of any third-party website. Accessing external links is at your own discretion and risk.
2.9 Platform Availability
We
endeavour
to keep
UniTracko

available at all times but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. We may perform scheduled maintenance, upgrades, or experience downtime beyond our reasonable control. We will communicate planned outages in advance where possible.
2.10 Limitation of Liability
To the fullest extent permitted by the laws of India,
UniTracko
, its founders, directors, employees, and partners shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from:
Use of or inability to use the platform
Decisions made on the basis of information from the platform
Missed application deadlines or lost opportunities
Unauthorised
access to your account due to your own negligence
Our total aggregate liability, if any, shall not exceed the amount paid by you to
UniTracko
in the 3 months preceding the claim.
2.11 Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

These Terms are governed by the laws of India. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms shall first be attempted to be resolved through good-faith negotiation. If unresolved within 30 days, disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of competent courts located in
Greater Noida
, India.
2.12 Changes to These Terms

We reserve the right to update these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-app notification at least 14 days before they take effect. Continued use of the platform after the effective date of changes constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
3. Cookie Policy
Last Updated: April 2026 | Effective Date: 1 April 2026 | Version
TL;DR — Plain English Summary
We use cookies to make the platform work and understand how it's being used.
We do not use cookies to serve ads or track you across other websites.
You can manage or delete cookies through your browser settings at any time.
3.1 What Are Cookies?
Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They serve various purposes
from keeping you logged in to helping us understand how visitors use the platform. Cookies cannot carry viruses or access other data on your device.
We also use similar technologies such as web beacons and local storage where appropriate. This policy covers all such technologies collectively referred to as 'cookies'.
3.2 Types of Cookies We Use
A. Essential Cookies (Always Active)

These are required for the platform to function correctly. They cannot be switched off. Without them, features such as logging in, saving your admission plan, or maintaining session security will not work.
Session cookies — expire when you close your browser
Authentication tokens — keep you securely logged in
Security cookies — protect against CSRF and session hijacking
Load-balancing cookies — ensure platform stability
B. Analytics & Performance Cookies
These help us understand how users interact with
UniTracko
so we can improve the experience. All data is
anonymised
before analysis — we cannot identify individual users from analytics data.
Google Analytics — page views, session duration, user flow, drop-off points
Performance monitoring — page load speed, error tracking
C. Preference Cookies
These remember your choices and settings to provide a more
personalised
experience.
Language or regional preferences
Saved search filters or dashboard configurations
3.3 What We Do NOT Use Cookies For
Targeted or
behavioural
advertising
Tracking you across third-party websites
Building advertising profiles
Selling cookie-derived data to advertisers or data brokers
3.4 Managing Your Cookie Preferences
You can control cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to:
View and delete cookies currently stored on your device
Block all cookies or only third-party cookies
Set preferences for future cookie acceptance
Please note: disabling essential cookies may significantly impact platform functionality. Disabling analytics cookies will not affect your ability to use the platform.
For guidance on managing cookies in your browser, visit the help documentation for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge respectively.
3.5 Third-Party Cookies
Some of our service providers (such as Google Analytics) may set their own cookies when you use
UniTracko
. These cookies are governed by those providers' own privacy and cookie policies, which we recommend you review. We do not control third-party cookies and are not responsible for them.
3.6 Updates to This Policy
We may update this Cookie Policy as our technology or applicable regulations change. We will notify users of significant changes. The most current version will always be available on our website.
4. Disclaimer
Last Updated: April 2026 | Effective Date: 1 April 2026 | Version
TL;DR — Plain English Summary
UniTracko
provides guidance
not guarantees.
Admission decisions are made by institutions, not by us.
Verify all critical details (cut-offs, deadlines, fees) directly with the institution.
This disclaimer protects you from acting on unverified information and protects us from unfair claims.
4.1 Nature of Information
All content on
UniTracko
including college profiles, cut-off data, eligibility criteria, scholarship information, career guidance, application timelines, and exam preparation resources
is provided for general informational and educational purposes only.

Nothing on this platform constitutes professional legal, financial, medical, or academic advisory services. If you require professional advice on any of these matters, please consult a qualified professional.
4.2 No Guarantee of Admission or Outcomes
UniTracko
provides planning and guidance tools to support
not decide
your admission journey. We explicitly disclaim any guarantee, warranty, or representation
egarding
Admission to any college, university, institution, or
programme
The accuracy, currency, or completeness of institution-specific data such as cut-offs, seat matrices, fee structures, or eligibility criteria
Outcomes of competitive examinations (JEE, NEET, CUET, CAT, CLAT, or others)
Career prospects, salaries, or professional outcomes following completion of any course
All admission decisions are made solely and entirely by the respective educational institutions, national or state counselling bodies, and relevant governing authorities.
UniTracko
has no influence over these decisions.
4.3 How We Source and Validate Information
UniTracko's
content team collects information from multiple sources to ensure it is as accurate and comprehensive as possible. Our sourcing process includes:
Official institution websites, admission portals, and published prospectuses
Government and regulatory body notifications (NTA, UGC, AICTE, state counselling authorities, etc.)
Publicly available official data such as
JoSAA
, CSAB, and state merit lists
Cross-referencing multiple credible sources before publishing any data
Regular review cycles to identify and update changed information
We are committed to making our information as accurate, exhaustive, and useful as possible. However, the nature of the education sector in India
with frequent, sometimes last-minute changes to cut-offs, eligibility criteria, seat matrices, fee structures, and deadlines
means that a small scope of ambiguity or inaccuracy will always exist, despite our best efforts.
Any incorrect information on the platform, if present, is entirely unintentional. We do not knowingly publish inaccurate data.
4.4 Accuracy, Currency, and Scope of Ambiguity

While we work diligently to keep all information accurate and up to date, we cannot guarantee that every piece of content is free from errors, omissions, or outdated details. Specific areas of inherent ambiguity include:
Cut-off data — opening and closing ranks change every year and are
finalised
only after each round of counselling
Seat availability — institutional intake capacity can be revised by regulatory bodies without advance notice
Fee structures — subject to revision by institutions, state governments, or regulatory bodies
Eligibility criteria — exam rules, reservation norms, and subject requirements are periodically revised
Application deadlines — frequently extended or revised by exam authorities without early notice
We update our platform as quickly as possible when we become aware of changes. However, the time between an official change and our platform update may result in temporarily outdated information.
Critical Rule: Always verify all important information
deadlines, eligibility, fees, and cut-offs
directly with the official institution, exam authority, or state counselling body before making any decision. Do not rely solely on any third-party platform, including
UniTracko
, for information on which a significant decision depends.
4.5 Exam Form Facilitation
Specific Accuracy Disclaimer
When
UniTracko
facilitates competitive exam form filling on behalf of a student, the information entered is based entirely on what the student and/or parent provides.
UniTracko
does not verify, authenticate, or take responsibility for:
The accuracy of personal details, academic records, or category claims provided by the student
Whether the student meets the eligibility criteria for the exam being applied for
Whether the exam authority accepts or rejects the application after submission
Students and parents must confirm all information before
authorising
submission. Please refer to Section 2.6 of the Terms & Conditions for the complete terms governing this service.
4.6 Third-Party Institutions and Data
Information about colleges, universities, courses, and exams on
UniTracko
is sourced from publicly available information, official websites, and institutional data.
UniTracko
is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or a representative of any educational institution, examination board, or government body unless explicitly stated.
References to specific institutions, rankings, or
programmes
are for informational purposes only and do not constitute a recommendation or endorsement.
4.7 External Links
UniTracko
may provide links to external websites including official college portals, government admission portals (such as
JoSAA
, CSAB, State CET cells), exam authority websites, and news sources. We are not responsible for:
The accuracy or reliability of content on external websites
The availability or uptime of third-party sites
Privacy practices of third-party sites
We recommend reviewing the privacy policy and terms of any external site you visit.
4.8 Platform Availability
We do not guarantee that
UniTracko
will be available continuously or without interruption. Technical issues, maintenance, server outages, or circumstances beyond our reasonable control may result in downtime.
UniTracko
shall not be liable for any loss resulting from platform unavailability.
4.9 Limitation of Liability
To the maximum extent permitted by applicable Indian law,
UniTracko
and its founders, directors, employees, consultants, and partners shall not be liable for:
Any decision — academic, financial, or personal — made on the basis of information from this platform
Consequences of unintentional errors, omissions, or ambiguities in platform content
Missed application deadlines, lost admission opportunities, or unsuccessful applications
Any outcome arising from the Form Facilitation Service, including consequences of information provided by the user
Any emotional, financial, or reputational impact arising from use of, or inability to use, the platform
Errors or inaccuracies in third-party data displayed on the platform
5. Our Data Promise
Written: April 2026 | This is a commitment, not a legal document.
This page exists because most privacy policies are written to protect the company, not you. Ours is different. This one is written for you.
Why We're Writing This
We know what happens when you fill a form on an education website in India.
Within minutes, your phone doesn't stop ringing. Colleges you've never heard of. Coaching
centres
you never asked about. Sales teams reading from scripts at 9pm.
You didn't consent to that. You just wanted information.
UniTracko
was built to be different. And we're putting that in writing.
What We Will NEVER Do
Sell, rent, or trade your personal data to any third party
ever, under any circumstances
Share your phone number with colleges, coaching
centres
, education agents, or any commercial entity who will call you uninvited
Share your email address for third-party promotional campaigns
Use your data to build an advertising profile or target you with ads
Hand your data to anyone who has paid to access it
Retain your data after you've asked us to delete it
Make it confusing or difficult to leave or delete your account
What We Will ALWAYS Do
Ask for your consent before we contact you and
honour
your answer immediately
Tell you clearly and in plain English what data we collect and exactly why
Use your data only to help you plan your admission journey
nothing else
Let you see all data we hold about you
just ask
Let you correct or delete your data at any time, no questions asked
Protect your information with industry-standard encryption and security
Be transparent if our data practices ever change
before they change
Tell you if something goes wrong
we will not hide data incidents from you
The Spam Call Problem
Our Position
This is the biggest trust issue in Indian EdTech. We are naming it directly.
In India, a common business model is to collect student data and sell it as 'leads' to colleges and coaching
centres
. The student fills one form and gets fifty calls.
That model funds platforms like the ones you've probably already had bad experiences with.
That is not
UniTracko's
model. It will never be
UniTracko's
model.
Your phone number is in our system for one reason: so we can help you when you need us. Not to be sold to the highest bidder.
You Are In Control
Always
Your data belongs to you. We are just holding it in trust.
Want to see exactly what we have? Ask —
privacy@unitracko.com
Want to correct something? Tell us. Done within 7 days.
Want to leave? Your account and all associated data will be deleted within 30 days.
Want to raise a concern? We'll respond within 48 hours.
No dark patterns. No confusing settings designed to trap you. No 'are you sure?' guilt loops repeated five times.
Our Commitment Under Indian Law
This promise aligns with and goes beyond our obligations under:
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and DPDP Rules, 2025
The Information Technology Act, 2000
The Consumer Protection Act, 2019
You have the right to complain to the Data Protection Board of India if you ever feel we are not living up to our obligations. We will cooperate fully with any such inquiry.
Questions? A Real Person Will Respond.
If you ever feel like we're not living up to this promise or if you just want to understand something better
we want to know.
Email:
privacy@unitracko.com
We read every message. We will reply.
"We collect only what we need. We never sell it. We never spam you. You can delete everything, anytime. That's it."
6. Refund & Cancellation Policy
Last Updated: April 2026 | Effective Date: 1 April 2026 | Version
TL;DR — Plain English Summary
Free plan: cancel any time, nothing to refund.
Subscriptions: all purchases are non-refundable. Please review your plan carefully before purchasing.
Form facilitation fee: non-refundable once work has commenced.
Cancelling a subscription is always simple
future renewals stop immediately.
Billing errors on our part will always be corrected.
6.1 Free Plan Users
UniTracko's
free plan is provided at no cost. There is nothing to refund. You may close your account at any time from your account settings, or by emailing
privacy@unitracko.com
. Your data will be permanently deleted within 30 days of your request.
6.2 Paid Subscriptions
Non-Refundable
All
UniTracko
subscription plans are non-refundable. By completing a subscription purchase, you acknowledge and agree that:
You have reviewed the features, duration, and pricing of the plan before purchasing
The subscription fee is charged upfront for the full subscription period
No refunds will be issued for unused days, weeks, or months remaining in a subscription period
No refunds will be issued if you choose not to use the platform after purchase
No refunds will be issued due to a change of mind after purchase
We encourage all users to explore our free plan thoroughly before upgrading to a paid subscription. If you have questions about what a paid plan includes, contact us at
billing@unitracko.com
before purchasing
we will be happy to clarify.
6.3 Form Facilitation Service Fee
Non-Refundable Once Work Commences
The service fee charged for
UniTracko's
Exam Form Facilitation Service is non-refundable once the facilitation work has commenced. 'Commencement' means the point at which
UniTracko's
team has begun the process of reviewing, preparing, or entering your form details.
Specifically, the following situations do not entitle you to a refund of the facilitation fee:
The exam authority rejects or does not process your application
You provided incorrect information and the form was submitted in accordance with your acknowledgement
The exam authority closes the application window before submission is complete, due to last-minute engagement on your part
You change your mind about the exam or course after work has commenced
A technical failure on the exam authority's portal prevents submission
The facilitation fee covers the service rendered by
UniTracko
not the outcome of the submission.
6.4 When a Refund May Be Considered
While our general policy is non-refundable,
UniTracko
will review and consider refund or credit requests in the following limited circumstances:
A duplicate charge occurred due to a technical or billing error on
UniTracko's
part
You were charged the wrong amount due to a pricing error on our platform
The Form Facilitation Service was paid for but
UniTracko
did not commence any work and did not engage with your form details in any way
UniTracko
experienced a platform-wide outage lasting more than 72 consecutive hours during your active subscription period, rendering the platform entirely inaccessible

In these cases, please contact billing@unitracko.com with documentation of the issue. We will review and respond within 5 business days. Approved refunds or credits will be processed within 7–10 business days.
A credit to your account (for future use on
UniTracko
) may be offered in lieu of a cash refund in certain cases.
6.5 How to Cancel a Subscription
You may cancel your subscription at any time. Cancellation is intentionally simple:
Email billing@unitracko.com with subject line 'Subscription Cancellation — [Your Registered Email]'

Cancellation takes effect immediately for future billing — you will not be charged for the next renewal cycle. Your subscription remains active and accessible until the end of the period you have already paid for.
Cancelling your subscription does not delete your account or data. To delete your account, email privacy@unitracko.com separately.
6.6 Auto-Renewal
All
UniTracko

subscriptions are set to auto-renew by default at the end of each subscription period, at the then-current price. You will receive an email reminder before each renewal. You may cancel auto-renewal at any time from your account settings.
UniTracko
is not responsible for missed cancellation if you did not receive the renewal reminder due to spam filters or a change in your registered email address.
6.7 Consumer Rights — India

This Refund Policy does not override any statutory rights you may have under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 (India). If you believe a charge was made in contravention of applicable consumer protection law, you may escalate your complaint to the relevant consumer forum or the National Consumer Helpline (NCH).
We will cooperate fully with any consumer dispute process.
6.8 Contact for Billing Issues
Email:
billing@unitracko.com
Subject line format: [Refund / Cancellation / Billing Error / Query] — [Your Registered Email]
We aim to respond to all billing queries within 1 business day.
— End of
UniTracko
Legal & Policy Documents —
Version 3.0 | April 2026 | www.unitracko.com | privacy@unitracko.com
This document should be reviewed by a qualified Indian legal professional before publishing.
